text: vv. 11, 12. These verses perhaps imply that God’s fidelity to his promises and God’s mercy have combined to restore the Jewish people; that his justice, satisfied with the expiation of their sins, no longer grudges them peace and well-being; that loyalty to the old covenant is once more a native growth in the land of Israel, and that God looks down to reward it. But the picture may be a more general one.
